category_title: Preschoolers who experience significant delays during activity transitions
minimal_context: Jose is deeply engaged in a classroom activity and is prompted by the teacher to transition to the next activity.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Jose, it is time to stop your current activity and move toward the next activity.

STUDENT: (Jose remains calm and deeply engages in his current activity.)

TUTOR: Please stop what you are doing and come over here.

STUDENT: (Jose does not stop his current activity and move toward the next activity.)

TUTOR: Jose, we need to go to the next activity now.

STUDENT: (Jose continues to take a long time to stop doing his activity, taking more than seven minutes to begin the transition.)

options.A: Ignore him and take the line of students to the next activity.
options.B: Give the student a verbal warning regarding his transition behavior.
options.C: Give positive attention and praise for following directions and listening.
options.D: Have a peer offer the transition choices to the student.
